Label: Statement
Description: The class of RDF statements (reified triples), each with a subject, predicate, and object.

Label: GTEx Experiment
Description: Associates a gene with a GTEx expression experiment measuring tissue and cell-specific expression and regulation across individuals, development, and species

Label: Compound
Description: A substance formed by chemical union of two or more elements or ingredients in definite proportion by weight.

Label: Site Annotation
Description: An annotation describing a specific site (residue position) on a protein sequence.

Label: PTM Annotation
Description: An annotation describing a post-translational modification of a protein.

Label: Protein
Description: A protein resource as defined by the UniProt core ontology.

Label: natural_variant_site
Description: Describes the natural sequence variants due to polymorphisms, disease-associated mutations, RNA editing and variations between strains, isolates or cultivars.

Label: Natural Variant Annotation
Description: An annotation describing a natural sequence variant of a protein.

Label: GO Term
Description: Associates a biological entity with a Gene Ontology term describing molecular function, biological process, or cellular component

Label: Gene
Description: A gene resource as defined by the UniProt core ontology.

Label: Complex
Description: A molecular entity formed by loose association involving two or more component molecular entities. The bonding between the components is normally weaker than in a covalent bond.

Label: Image
Description: An image or picture generated in an experiment by a device. For example, a microscopic image with metadata.

Label: Gene Signature
Description: A form of gene expression profiling that is indicative of the biological behavior of a tumor or cancer. This pattern of expression is comprised of unique clusters of genes that display differential levels of expression. The technology is based on microarray analysis and allows for accurate prognostication based upon these criteria.

Label: Disease
Description: A disease resource as defined by the UniProt core ontology.

Label: Metabolite
Description: Any substance involved in metabolism, either as a product of metabolism or as necessary for metabolism.

Label: Drug
Description: A chemical or biologic substance, used as a medical therapy, that has a physiological effect on an organism.

Label: isoform
Description: A quality of a protein that harbors a variation from the native protein (called canonical form), which could arise due to alternative promoter usage, alternative splicing, alternative translation initiation and ribosomal frameshifting.

Label: Clinical Evidence
Description: Documentation supporting direct medical treatment or testing.

Label: Pathway
Description: A biological pathway as defined by the UniProt core ontology.

Label: domain
Description: A structural or functional domain of a protein.

Label: Experiment
Description: Experiment is an entity which describes the collective activity conducted in a science lab.

Label: citation
Description: A bibliographic citation associated with a UniProt entity.

Label: MolecularEntity
Description: Any constitutionally or isotopically distinct atom, molecule, ion, ion pair, radical, radical ion, complex, conformer etc., identifiable as a separately distinguishable entity.

Label: annotation
Description: An annotation describing a feature or property of a UniProt entity.

Label: experimental conditions
Description: A state of being, an external or environmental factor or a treatment observed or administered prior to or concurrent with an investigative procedure such as an assessment of a morphological or physiological state or property in a single individual or sample or in a group of individuals or samples, especially a state, factor or treatment which has the potential to influence the outcome of such an assessment.

Label: NegativePropertyAssertion
Description: The class of negative property assertions, stating that an individual does not have a given property value.

Label: Protein Family
Description: Collection of proteins that are evolutionarily related.  This is reflected in the structural and functional similarities as well as in the extent of sequence conservation or residue identity. (from SCOP)

Label: anatomical entity
Description: Biological entity that is either an individual member of a biological species or constitutes the structural organization of an individual member of a biological species.

Label: assertion
Description: A statement made by a particular agent on a particular occasion that a particular proposition is true, based on the evaluation of one or more lines of evidence.

Label: perturbagen
Description: The role of a molecular entity (small molecule, siRNA/shRNA, etc.) in altering the activity of proteins that serve as targets in a bioassay.

Label: Feature
Description: A prominent or identifiable aspect, region, or site of interest.

Label: protein complex group
Description: Genes whose protein products form a macromolecular complex (GO:0043234).

Label: Subcellular Location
Description: A subcellular location to which a protein is localized.

Label: Property
Description: The class of RDF properties.

Label: Amino Acid
Description: Any organic compounds containing amino (-NH2) and carboxyl (-COOH) groups.  In biochemistry, used to refer to the twenty-plus L-alpha-amino acids found in proteins.

Label: Class
Description: The class of OWL classes.

Label: cell line
Description: A cultured cell population that is genetically stable and homogeneous, sharing a common propagation history through successive passages in culture.

Label: Dataset
Description: A set of RDF triples that are published, maintained or aggregated by a single provider (VoID dataset).

Label: Class
Description: The class of RDF classes.

Label: Source
Description: A related resource from which the described resource is derived.

Label: type
Description: The subject is an instance of a class.

Label: object
Description: The object of the reified RDF statement.

Label: subject
Description: The subject of the reified RDF statement.

Label: predicate
Description: The predicate of the reified RDF statement.

Label: provider
Description: The service provider, service operator, or service performer; the goods producer.

Label: expressed in
Description: x expressed in y if and only if there is a gene expression process (GO:0010467) that occurs in y, and one of the following holds: (i) x is a gene, and x is transcribed into a transcript as part of the gene expression process (ii) x is a transcript, and the transcription of x is part of the gene expression process (iii) x is a mature gene product such as a protein, and x was translated or otherwise processed from a transcript that was transcribed as part of this gene expression process

Label: label
Description: A human-readable name for the subject.

Label: evidence
Description: An evidence code or annotation supporting an assertion.

Label: gene expression value
Description: A gene expression value is a measured value obtained from a gene expression experiment.

Label: comment
Description: A description of the subject resource.

Label: PubMed ID
Description: The PubMed identifier (PMID) of a referenced publication.

Label: associated with
Description: Expresses a relationship between two named things where the relationship is typically generated statistically (though not in all cases), and is weaker than its child, 'correlated with', but stronger than its parent, 'related to'.

Label: target for
Description: A gene is a target of a disease when its products are druggable and when a drug interaction with the gene product could have a therapeutic effect

Label: interacts with
Description: A relationship that holds between two entities in which the processes executed by the two entities are causally connected.

Label: has evidence
Description: inverse of is evidence for

Label: Date
Description: A point or period of time associated with an event in the lifecycle of the resource.

Label: provided by
Description: The value in this node property represents the knowledge provider that created or assembled the node and all of its attributes.

Label: position
Description: The sequence position of a feature or site.

Label: Protein residue
Description: Data on a single amino acid residue position in a protein structure.

Label: Type
Description: The nature or genre of the resource.

Label: involved in or involved in regulation of
Description: c involved in or regulates p if and only if either (i) c is involved in p or (ii) c is involved in regulation of p

Label: Site Annotation
Description: An annotation describing a specific site (residue position) on a protein sequence.

Label: activity
Description: Relates a catalytic activity or enzyme to the protein that carries it out.

Label: axiom has evidence
Description: A relationship between a sentence and an instance of a piece of evidence in which the evidence supports the axiom

Label: identifier
Description: The identifier property represents any kind of identifier for any kind of Thing.

Label: therapeutic response
Description: A positive response to a treatment or intervention

Label: parenteral administration
Description: Administration of drugs or other substances by routes other than the digestive tract.

Label: chembl natural product
Description: Flag indicating if a chemical entity is a natural product.

Label: molecular weight
Description: Molecular weight in g/mol, determined from molecular formula or sequence.

Label: smiles
Description: A specification in form of a line notation for describing the structure of chemical species using short ASCII strings (SMILES).

Label: InChI key string
Description: A hashed, fixed-length form of the IUPAC International Chemical Identifier (InChI key) for a chemical structure.

Label: exactMatch
Description: Links two concepts with a high degree of confidence that they can be used interchangeably.

Label: chembl availability type
Description: Text indicating the availability type of the chemical entity.

Label: has sequence location
Description: holds between two nucleic acid entities when the subject can be localized in sequence coordinates on the object. For example, between an exon and a chromosome/contig.

Label: description
Description: A description of the item.

Label: Log2 Ratio
Description: Log2 ratio of expression between conditions

Label: Delta Log2 Ratio
Description: Delta log2 fold-change ratio between conditions

Label: affected by
Description: describes an entity of which the state or quality is affected by another existing entity.

Label: participates in
Description: a relation between a continuant and a process, in which the continuant is somehow involved in the process

Label: Molecule
Description: A molecule resource as defined by the UniProt core ontology.

Label: involved in
Description: c involved_in p if and only if c enables some process p', and p' is part of p

Label: organism
Description: The organism in which the entity occurs.

Label: Accession Number
Description: A control number unique to an object.  It is used to identify the object among other objects in a collection.

Label: negatively regulates
Description: p negatively regulates q iff p regulates q, and p decreases the rate or magnitude of execution of q.

Label: positively regulates
Description: p positively regulates q iff p regulates q, and p increases the rate or magnitude of execution of q.

Label: taxon
Description: The taxonomic classification of an organism.

Label: mnemonic
Description: A short mnemonic identifier for a UniProt entry.

Label: sequence
Description: The amino acid or nucleotide sequence of the entity.

Label: length
Description: The length of a sequence.

Label: Peptide molecular weights
Description: List of molecular weight(s) of one or more proteins or peptides, for example cut by proteolytic enzymes or reagents.

Label: Has marker gene
Description: Associates a gene or protein with marker gene expression

Label: enables
Description: c enables p iff c is capable of p and c acts to execute p.

Label: has_variant_part
Description: A relation between a sequence entity (i.e. a sequence, feature, or qualified feature) and a part of this entity that is variant in terms of its sequence, position, or expression.

Label: category
Description: A category for the item. Greater signs or slashes can be used to informally indicate a category hierarchy.

Label: has sequence variant
Description: Holds between a genomic entity and a sequence variant of it.

Label: Molecular Sequence Variation Type
Description: The category or type of variation or abnormality present in an amino acid or nucleic acid sequence.

Label: Alternative Sequence Annotation
Description: An annotation describing an alternative sequence (e.g. splice variant) of a protein.

Label: part of
Description: Relates an entity to a larger entity of which it is a part.

Label: subClassOf
Description: The subject is a subclass of a class.

Label: Description
Description: An account of the resource.

Label: produces
Description: a produces b if some process that occurs_in a has_output b, where a and b are material entities. Examples: hybridoma cell line produces monoclonal antibody reagent; chondroblast produces avascular GAG-rich matrix.

Label: Indication
Description: A sign, signal, circumstance, or symptom which serves to indicate or point out the cause, pathology, treatment, or issue of an attack of disease; the basis for initiation of a treatment for a disease or of a diagnostic test (causal, or symptomatic, or disease-specific indication).

Label: feature type
Description: Property of a subsequence that may interfere with the binding of a molecule.

Label: annotation
Description: An annotation describing a feature or property of a UniProt entity.

Label: localized
Description: A spatial pattern quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being confined or restricted to a particular location.

Label: Is Top Prediction
Description: Flag indicating whether this subcellular localization is the top-ranked prediction for the entity

Label: has OBO namespace
Description: The OBO namespace to which a term belongs.

Label: domain
Description: A structural or functional domain of a protein.

Label: similar to
Description: holds between an entity and some other entity with similar features.

Label: EC number
Description: An Enzyme Commission (EC) number of an enzyme.

Label: catalyzes
Description: Holds between a macromolecular machine (typically an enzyme or ribozyme) and a biochemical reaction or process whose rate it accelerates, without itself being consumed, by lowering the activation energy.

Label: Stained Appearance
Description: Marked or discolored by foreign material.

Label: Well
Description: A Well is a component of the Well/Plate/Screen construct to describe screening applications. A Well has a number of WellSample elements that link to the Images collected in this well.

Label: chromosome
Description: A structure composed of a very long molecule of DNA and associated proteins (e.g. histones) that carries hereditary information.

Label: part of
Description: a core relation that holds between a part and its whole

Label: Directory name
Description: The name of a directory.

Label: is variant of
Description: is variant of: a relationship indicating that two entities are different (by some measure), but either achieve the same objectives in different ways or are permutations of one another (temporal, logical or otherwise)

Label: supporting
Description: An evidence direction indicating that an evidence line provides an argument that supports the validity of a target assertion.

Label: encodes
Description: A relation between two objects, in which the first object contains information that is used to produce the second object.

Label: direct interaction
Description: Interaction between molecules that are in direct contact with each other.

Label: Marked
Description: A prominent or noticeable characteristic.

Label: acronym
Description: A synonym type for describing abbreviations that are a part of the full name's words, such as initialisms or alphabetisms.

Label: Isoform
Description: Refers to variants of the same protein which can be separated on special conducting media using electrophoresis. The differences may arise from genetically determined differences in primary structure or by modification of the same primary sequence.

Label: causally influences
Description: The entity or characteristic A is causally upstream of the entity or characteristic B, A having an effect on B. An entity corresponds to any biological type of entity as long as a mass is measurable. A characteristic corresponds to a particular specificity of an entity (e.g., phenotype, shape, size).

Label: is active in
Description: c executes activity in d if and only if c enables p and p occurs_in d.  Assuming no action at a distance by gene products, if a gene product enables (is capable of) a process that occurs in some structure, it must have at least some part in that structure.

Label: id
Description: A unique identifier for an entity. Must be either a CURIE shorthand for a URI or a complete URI

Label: MolecularEntity
Description: Any constitutionally or isotopically distinct atom, molecule, ion, ion pair, radical, radical ion, complex, conformer etc., identifiable as a separately distinguishable entity.

Label: Clinical Significance
Description: A finding that has treatment or management implications for a patient's condition.

Label: variant_origin
Description: A quality inhering in a variant by virtue of its origin.

Label: is cited by
Description: The cited entity is cited by the citing entity.

Label: citation
Description: A bibliographic citation associated with a UniProt entity.

Label: disease
Description: Relates an entity to a disease it is associated with.

Label: molecularly interacts with
Description: An interaction relationship in which the two partners are molecular entities that directly physically interact with each other for example via a stable binding interaction or a brief interaction during which one modifies the other.

Label: isoform
Description: A quality of a protein that harbors a variation from the native protein (called canonical form), which could arise due to alternative promoter usage, alternative splicing, alternative translation initiation and ribosomal frameshifting.

Label: InChI string
Description: An IUPAC International Chemical Identifier (InChI) string describing a chemical structure.

Label: up-regulates activity
Description: The effect of a modulator entity A on a modulated entity B that increases the frequency, rate or extent of the molecular function of B, an elemental biological activity occurring at the molecular level, such as catalysis or binding (GO:0044093).

Label: correlated with condition
Description: A relationship between an entity and a condition (phenotype or disease) with which it exhibits a statistical dependence relationship.

Label: actively involved in
Description: holds between a continuant and a process or function, where the continuant actively contributes to part or all of the process or function it realizes

Label: Gene Symbol
Description: A unique gene name approved by an organism specific nomenclature committee.

Label: Approval
Description: Acceptance as satisfactory by an authoritative body; established by authority; given authoritative approval.

Label: is annotation of
Description: is annotation of is a relation between some textual entity and the entity that it annotates.

Label: down-regulates activity
Description: The effect of a modulator entity A on a modulated entity B that decreases the frequency, rate or extent of the molecular function of B, an elemental biological activity occurring at the molecular level, such as catalysis or binding (GO:0044093).

Label: homologous to
Description: holds between two biological entities that have common evolutionary origin

Label: isomeric SMILES descriptor
Description: An isomeric SMILES descriptor is a SMILES descriptor that is written with isotopic and chiral specifications.

Label: used_in
Description: A relation connecting an evidence type to an assertion method where the assertion method describes the type of process employed to evaluate the evidence.

Label: acts upstream of or within
Description: c acts upstream of or within p if c is enables f, and f is causally upstream of or within p. c is a material entity and p is an process.

Label: status
Description: The status of the study (enumerated).

Label: count
Description: A count value annotation in oboInOwl.

Label: score
Description: The score of an experimentally derived feature such as a p-value.

Label: Lipid Droplets
Description: Dynamic cytoplasmic organelles found in almost all cells. They consist of a central core of LIPIDS surrounded by a phospholipid monolayer studded with surface proteins, and are involved in LIPID METABOLISM and storage.

Label: Focal Adhesions
Description: An anchoring junction of the cell to a non-cellular substrate. It is composed of a specialized area of the plasma membrane where bundles of the ACTIN CYTOSKELETON terminate and attach to the transmembrane linkers, INTEGRINS, which in turn attach through their extracellular domains to EXTRACELLULAR MATRIX PROTEINS.

Label: Peroxisomes
Description: Microbodies which occur in animal and plant cells and in certain fungi and protozoa. They contain peroxidase, catalase, and allied enzymes. (From Singleton and Sainsbury, Dictionary of Microbiology and Molecular Biology, 2nd ed)

Label: Golgi Apparatus
Description: A stack of flattened vesicles that functions in posttranslational processing and sorting of proteins, receiving them from the rough ENDOPLASMIC RETICULUM and directing them to secretory vesicles, LYSOSOMES, or the CELL MEMBRANE. The movement of proteins takes place by transfer vesicles that bud off from the rough endoplasmic reticulum or Golgi apparatus and fuse with the Golgi, lysosomes or cell membrane. (From Glick, Glossary of Biochemistry and Molecular Biology, 1990)

Label: kinetochore
Description: A multisubunit complex that is located at the centromeric region of DNA and provides an attachment point for the spindle microtubules.

Label: nucleoplasm
Description: That part of the nuclear content other than the chromosomes or the nucleolus.

Label: Mitotic Chromosome
Description: Condensed chromosomes observed during the M phase of the cell cycle.  Individual chromosomes can be discerned and differentiated morphologically.

Label: Mitochondrion
Description: Semiautonomous, self-reproducing organelles that occur in the cytoplasm of all cells of most, but not all, eukaryotes. Mitochondria are the principal energy source of the cell and contain the cytochrome enzymes of terminal electron transport and the enzymes of the citric acid cycle, fatty acid oxidation, and oxidative phosphorylation.

Label: negative
Description: Indicates that an annotation or assertion is negated.

Label: Endosomes
Description: Cytoplasmic vesicles formed when COATED VESICLES shed their CLATHRIN coat. Endosomes internalize macromolecules bound by receptors on the cell surface.

Label: nucleolus
Description: A small, dense body one or more of which are present in the nucleus of eukaryotic cells. It is rich in RNA and protein, is not bounded by a limiting membrane, and is not seen during mitosis.

Label: Lysosomes
Description: A class of morphologically heterogeneous cytoplasmic particles in animal and plant tissues characterized by their content of hydrolytic enzymes and the structure-linked latency of these enzymes. The intracellular functions of lysosomes depend on their lytic potential. The single unit membrane of the lysosome acts as a barrier between the enzymes enclosed in the lysosome and the external substrate. The activity of the enzymes contained in lysosomes is limited or nil unless the vesicle in which they are enclosed is ruptured or undergoes MEMBRANE FUSION. (From Rieger et al., Glossary of Genetics: Classical and Molecular, 5th ed).

Label: mitotic spindle
Description: A spindle that forms as part of mitosis. Mitotic and meiotic spindles contain distinctive complements of proteins associated with microtubules.

Label: plasma membrane
Description: The membrane surrounding a cell that separates the cell from its external environment. It consists of a phospholipid bilayer and associated proteins.

Label: centrosome
Description: A structure comprised of a core structure (in most organisms, a pair of centrioles) and peripheral material from which a microtubule-based structure, such as a spindle apparatus, is organized.

Label: cytosol
Description: The part of the cytoplasm that does not contain organelles but which does contain other particulate matter, such as protein complexes.

Label: aggresome
Description: An inclusion body formed by dynein-dependent retrograde transport of an aggregated protein on microtubules.

Label: midbody
Description: A thin cytoplasmic bridge formed between daughter cells at the end of cytokinesis. The midbody forms where the contractile ring constricts, and may persist for some time before finally breaking to complete cytokinesis.

Label: vesicle
Description: Any small, fluid-filled, spherical organelle enclosed by membrane.

Label: cleavage furrow
Description: The cleavage furrow is a plasma membrane invagination at the cell division site. The cleavage furrow begins as a shallow groove and eventually deepens to divide the cytoplasm.

Label: centriolar satellite
Description: A small (70-100 nm) cytoplasmic granule that contains a number of centrosomal proteins; centriolar satellites traffic toward microtubule minus ends and are enriched near the centrosome.

Label: annotatedProperty
Description: The property being annotated in an OWL axiom or annotation.

Label: Medical Subject Headings
Description: Controlled vocabulary thesaurus produced by the NATIONAL LIBRARY OF MEDICINE. It consists of sets of terms naming descriptors in a hierarchical structure that permits searching at various levels of specificity.

Label: part of
Description: Relates an entity to a larger entity of which it is a part.

Label: Top Class
Description: Top class ID for cell medoids

Label: UMAP X
Description: UMAP X coordinate for cell medoids

Label: UMAP Y
Description: UMAP Y coordinate for cell medoids

Label: causally upstream of
Description: p is causally upstream of q iff p is causally related to q, the end of p precedes the end of q, and p is not an occurrent part of q.

Label: source
Description: The source of the information about the entity.

Label: name
Description: The name of the item.

Label: taxonID
Description: An identifier for the set of taxon information (a Darwin Core taxon).

Label: has component
Description: w 'has component' p if w 'has part' p and w is such that it can be directly disassembled into n parts p, p2, p3, ..., pn, where these parts are of similar type.

Label: p-value
Description: A quantitative confidence value that represents the probability of obtaining a result at least as extreme as that actually obtained, assuming that the actual value was the result of chance alone.

Label: conditionsOfAccess
Description: Conditions that affect the availability of, or method(s) of access to, an item.

Label: Signed Log10 P-Value
Description: Signed log10 p-value for a treatment effect

Label: Higher Level In
Description: Higher level in relation for a treatment effect

Label: Gene Alias
Description: This object holds alternative names for a gene.

Label: colocalizes with
Description: a colocalizes_with b if and only if  a is transiently or peripherally associated with b[GO].

Label: Species Group Flag
Description: Flag indicating if a complex or family is a species group

Label: wasAttributedTo
Description: Attribution is the ascribing of an entity to an agent.

Label: Therapy Used at Visit
Description: An indication of the type of therapy used during a visit.

Label: Identifier
Description: An unambiguous reference to the resource within a given context.

Label: scientific name
Description: The scientific name of an organism.

Label: acts upstream of
Description: c acts upstream of p if and only if c enables some f that is involved in p' and p' occurs chronologically before p, is not part of p, and affects the execution of p. c is a material entity and f, p, p' are processes.

Label: Has marker gene in kidney
Description: Associates a gene or protein with marker gene expression in kidney tissue

Label: Summary
Description: A shortened version of a speech or written text, containing only the main points.

Label: file
Description: A file is an information-bearing object that contains a physical embodiment of some information using a particular character encoding.

Label: contains
Description: A contains B iff the spatial region occupied by A has the spatial region occupied by B as a part, and B is not part of A.

Label: database cross-reference
Description: A database cross-reference relating a term to an equivalent or related entry in an external database.

Label: additionalType
Description: An additional type for the item, typically used for adding more specific types from external vocabularies.

Label: causally related to
Description: relation that links two events, processes, states, or objects such that one event, process, state, or object (a cause) contributes to the production of another event, process, state, or object (an effect) where the cause is partly or wholly responsible for the effect, and the effect is partly or wholly dependent on the cause.

Label: Has marker gene in liver
Description: Associates a gene or protein with marker gene expression in liver tissue

Label: Has marker gene in heart
Description: Associates a gene or protein with marker gene expression in heart tissue

Label: start coordinate
Description: The position at which the subject genomic entity starts on the chromosome or other entity to which it is located on. (ie: the start of the sequence being referenced is 1).

Label: assertion
Description: A statement made by a particular agent on a particular occasion that a particular proposition is true, based on the evaluation of one or more lines of evidence.

Label: canonical SMILES descriptor
Description: A canonical SMILES descriptor is a SMILES descriptor that is produced using a canonicalization algorithm which results in one special generic SMILES among all valid possibilities.

Label: acts upstream of, positive effect
Description: c 'acts upstream of, positive effect' p if c is enables f, and f is causally upstream of p, and the direction of f is positive

Label: reference
Description: An entity which has used another entity as reference.

Label: Protein variants
Description: Protein sequence variants produced e.g. from alternative splicing, alternative promoter usage, alternative initiation and ribosomal frameshifting.

Label: mutation
Description: A change in a sequence or structure in comparison to a reference entity due to a  insertion, deletion or substitution event.

Label: acts upstream of or within, positive effect
fused with
Description: A structural quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being merged with another entity.

Label: acts upstream of, negative effect
Description: c 'acts upstream of, negative effect' p if c is enables f, and f is causally upstream of p, and the direction of f is negative

Label: closeMatch
Description: Links two concepts that are sufficiently similar that they can be used interchangeably in some information retrieval applications.

Label: Recommends Therapy
Description: An assertion recommends a particular therapy

Label: Guideline
Description: A systematic statement of policy rules or principles. Guidelines may be developed by government agencies at any level, institutions, professional societies, governing boards, or by convening expert panels.

Label: Version Number Text
Description: A numeric character string that identifies a form or variant of a type or original.

Label: rangeIncludes
Description: Relates a property to a class that constitutes (one of) the expected type(s) for values of the property.

Label: domainIncludes
Description: Relates a property to a class that is (one of) the type(s) the property is expected to be used on.

Label: used
Description: Usage is the beginning of utilizing an entity by an activity.

Label: VariO term
Description: A Variation Ontology term associated with a sequence variant.

Label: acts upstream of or within, negative effect
subPropertyOf
Description: The subject is a subproperty of a property.

Label: assesses
Description: The item being described is intended to assess the competency or learning outcome defined by the referenced term.

Label: version
Description: The version of the entity or record.

Label: isDefinedBy
Description: The definition of the subject resource.

Label: MD5 checksum
Description: An MD5 checksum of the sequence.

Label: Study Name
Description: The name applied to a scientific investigation.

Label: target
Description: Indicates a target EntryPoint, or url, for an Action.

Label: type
Description: The type of the item.

Label: Disease or Disorder
Description: Any abnormal condition of the body or mind that causes discomfort, dysfunction, or distress to the person affected or those in contact with the person. The term is often used broadly to include injuries, disabilities, syndromes, symptoms, deviant behaviors, and atypical variations of structure and function.

Label: Analytical Fraction
Description: The portion of a specimen that was isolated from the whole and collected via a fractionation process to be subjected to further analysis.

Label: species
Description: The species rank in the NCBI Taxonomy.

Label: class
Description: The rdfs:Class that is the type of all entities in a class-based partition of a VoID dataset.

Label: class partition
Description: A subset of a VoID dataset that contains only the entities of a certain rdfs:Class.

Label: distinct objects
Description: The total number of distinct objects in a VoID dataset.

Label: distinct subjects
Description: The total number of distinct subjects in a VoID dataset.

Label: entities
Description: The total number of entities that are described in a VoID dataset.

Label: properties
Description: The total number of distinct properties in a VoID dataset.

Label: property
Description: The property that is the predicate of all triples in a property-based partition of a VoID dataset.

Label: property partition
Description: A subset of a VoID dataset that contains only the triples of a certain property.

Label: triples
Description: The total number of triples contained in a VoID dataset.

Label: datatype
Description: The datatype of the literals in a datatype-based partition (VoID extension).

Label: datatype partition
Description: A subset of a VoID dataset that contains only the literals of a certain datatype (VoID extension).

Label: object class partition
Description: A subset of a VoID dataset partitioned by the rdfs:Class of the objects of its triples (VoID extension).

Label: modified
Description: Date on which the resource was changed.

Label: last updated on
Description: The date on which the resource was last updated (PAV provenance, authoring and versioning ontology).

Label: version
Description: The version number of a resource (PAV provenance, authoring and versioning ontology).

Label: Top Class Name
Description: Top class name for cell medoids.
